channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Florida’s ‘Alligator Alcatraz’ migrant detention centre to close
item.description: The US is set to shut down the federal migrant detention centre known as ‘Alligator Alcatraz’.

item.title: Raid on Indian opposition leader triggers violence in Kerala
item.description: Raid on Indian opposition leader triggers violence in Kerala

item.title: India orders demolition drive along border as Pakistan tensions simmer
item.description: The interior minister urged officials to address infiltration, narcotics, smuggling, and other 'trans-border crimes'.

item.title: Russia to task bankers with shooting down Ukrainian drones
item.description: New legislation aims to integrate banks into Russia's air defences using jamming systems and trained employees.

item.title: EU states summon Russian envoys over Kyiv threat
item.description: Anger of EU states won't help smooth the way for Brussels to mediate a ceasefire, an idea Putin now says he accepts.

item.title: Iran government sells subsidised meat for Eid al-Adha under blockade
item.description: Iranians are seeing the price of meat, and other basics, escalate due to the US blockade and sanctions.

item.title: Canada chooses Swedish early warning planes rather than US model
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Carney has said he wants to diversify from the US which has threatened to annex Canada.

item.title: Rescue teams find five of seven trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven Lao ​nationals entered the cave in Xaisomboun province last week before a landslide blocked their ⁠exit.

item.title: US, Iran issue conflicting reports on deal as Trump says no sanction relief
item.description: US president says Iran can't 'out-wait' him, suggesting that Washington is not satisfied with Tehran's offers for deal.

item.title: Indonesian authorities crack down on controversial Papua documentary
item.description: Indonesian authorities have shut down several screenings of a new documentary about alleged human rights abuses in Papua

item.title: FIFA subpoenaed by New York, New Jersey in World Cup ticket investigation
item.description: US States react following increasing criticism of football's global governing body for the pricing of 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Eid celebrations in Gaza overshadowed by Israeli attacks
item.description: Palestinians across Gaza marked Eid al-Adha prayers amongst rubble and destruction as Israeli attacks continued.

item.title: Will the AI race fuel another boom or another bubble?
item.description: AI giants are racing towards public listings as valuations soar.

item.title: Family mourn ‘Hamas leader’ killed in Israeli attack
item.description: A funeral has been held for Mohammed Odeh, believed to be the leader of Hamas’s military wing.

item.title: Mogadishu gathers for Eid with prayers, family meals and outings
item.description: Mogadishu has been tentatively emerging from the waves of violence that have rocked the city over recent decades.

item.title: Muslims worldwide celebrate Eid al-Adha, the Festival of Sacrifice
item.description: The sacred holiday coincides with Hajj, drawing over 1.7 million pilgrims seeking spiritual fulfilment in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Why is Israel attacking Lebanon’s Nabatieh, the major southern city?
item.description: Israel issued forced displacement for Lebanon's southern city of Nabatieh on Tuesday. Why is it focused on this city?

item.title: Bangladesh seeks IMF aid: How badly has Iran war hit its economy?
item.description: The International Monetary Fund warns that Iran war could trigger a spike in global debt levels.

item.title: What is the Lobito Corridor, cited by US Africa envoy as model for ties?
item.description: Washington is set on a trade-focused shift in Africa, but critics say it facilitates further plundering of resources.

item.title: Rescue teams celebrate as survivors found in Laos cave
item.description: Rescue teams celebrate as survivors found in Laos cave

item.title: Who is Moise Kouame, the youngest French Open match winner since 1991?
item.description: World no 318's victory over Marin Cilic also made him the youngest man to claim a Grand Slam victory in 17 years.

item.title: The Baltics urgently need a de-escalation mechanism; Belarus can help
item.description: Amid growing risk of spillover from the Ukraine war, the Baltic states could seek de-escalation channels through Minsk.

item.title: Nigeria’s Eid Crisis: When a ram becomes a luxury
item.description: At a Nigerian sheep market, human rights advocate Sadaatu Madaki says that rising prices make Eid difficult for families

item.title: DRC facing ‘catastrophic collision’ of Ebola and war, WHO chief warns
item.description: 'Stopping this Ebola transmission depends entirely on humanitarian access,' said Tedros, the WHO chief.

item.title: ‘Eid does not enter tents’: Palestinians in Gaza face grim holiday
item.description: Rising costs and displacement erase Eid traditions, leaving Gaza residents struggling with sorrow and economic hardship.
